mechanism_narrative: The prevailing level of the federal funds rate serves as the baseline risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows; lower rate levels mechanically increase present values and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between FEDFUNDS levels and 20-day forward returns is greater than -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating the expected inverse relationship is absent.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4% signals an overheated labor market where accelerating wage growth compresses corporate profit margins and increases the probability of monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated CPIAUCSL with near-zero FEDFUNDS generates deeply negative real interest rates that erode fixed-income alternatives and channel capital into equities as inflation hedges, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction signal produces a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or joint real-rate proxies fail to predict positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment exceeds 150,000 thousand, the level and momentum of PAYEMS reflect a tight labor market where continued hiring signals robust demand rather than cost-push overheating, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150000, or the mean return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures immediate consumer demand impulses that translate into near-term revenue surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 102 signals a contraction in manufacturing and goods-producing sectors that precedes earnings downgrades and credit spread widening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates below 25 basis points reflect extreme monetary accommodation that suppresses short-term funding volatility and induces rapid risk-appetite flows into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns during triggered episodes are not significantly positive versus zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm a broad-based demand recovery where consumer spending and residential investment reinforce each other, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or instances with jointly positive changes do not yield positive returns in at least 55% of cases.
